contest.title: Neowise Labs Contest 1 (Codeforces Round 1018, Div. 1 + Div. 2)

sources.notes: Statement and constraints: count all XOR sums of choices from intervals [l_i,r_i] for m <= 18, total n <= 2e5, and total output domain size <= 2^18.
Official tutorial derives XOR convolution via FWHT, transforms interval polynomials A_i, simplifies prefix sums using the lowbit of k, and reduces each factor to a two-term polynomial B_i.
Luogu solution emphasizes the prefix-FWT cancellation by lowbit(k), the construction of G_{i,p}, and the idea of computing products of two-term set power series.
Independent Luogu writeup describes decomposing intervals by lowbit class and converting each interval contribution into a_i x^{p_i} + b_i x^{q_i}, then merging equal exponents and applying FWT.
Cnblogs solution cross-checks the closed form for prefix FWT sums, the product of (a_i + b_i x^{c_i}) factors, and an O(nm + m2^m) implementation.

annotation.summary: For every XOR value x, count the number of ways to choose one integer a_i from each interval [l_i,r_i] such that the XOR of all chosen values is x, then output the XOR of f_x * 2^x modulo 998244353 over all x.
annotation.constraints: n up to 2e5, m <= 18, total n <= 2e5 and total 2^m <= 2^18. Directly building every interval's length-2^m transform would be too slow.
annotation.core_idea: View each interval as a set polynomial A_i = x^{l_i}+...+x^{r_i}. The desired counts are the XOR convolution A_1 star A_2 star ... star A_n, so after XOR-FWT we need pointwise products of F(A_i). For a transform index k, F(A_i)[k] is a signed prefix difference f(k,r_i)-f(k,l_i-1), where f(k,r)=sum_{0<=j<=r}(-1)^{popcount(k&j)}. If p=lowbit(k), complete blocks of length 2^{p+1} cancel, so the prefix contribution depends only on the high part floor(k/2^{p+1}) and one lowbit-derived coefficient. For each fixed p, every interval contribution becomes the FWT of a two-term polynomial a_i x^{c_i}+b_i x^{d_i}. Normalize it to a_i+b_i x^{c_i xor d_i}, merge factors with the same exponent, compute the product by a FWHT/SOS-style transform, place the obtained pointwise values for all k with lowbit p, and finally inverse XOR-FWT to get all f_x.
annotation.complexity: O(nm + m2^m) to O(nm^2 + m2^m) depending on the interval decomposition formulation; O(2^m) memory aside from input
tricks: Turn XOR-sum counting into XOR convolution of interval set polynomials.
Use XOR-FWT so n-fold XOR convolution becomes pointwise multiplication.
Compute interval transform values through prefix differences instead of materializing each interval polynomial.
Exploit cancellation over full blocks determined by lowbit(k).
Reduce each fixed-lowbit contribution to products of two-term set polynomials.

solution_variants.name: lowbit-prefix-fwt-two-term-products
solution_variants.summary: Enumerate the least significant set bit p of the transform index k. For every interval, derive the two coefficients and two high-part exponents describing F(A_i)[k]. Normalize and merge equal exponents, evaluate the product of all two-term factors with a FWHT-like pass, fill the transformed answer positions for this p, handle k=0 separately, then inverse FWT.

solution_variants.name: dyadic-interval-decomposition
solution_variants.summary: An equivalent derivation decomposes each [l_i,r_i] into aligned power-of-two blocks. For transform indices with fixed lowbit, only blocks small enough to avoid cancellation contribute, yielding at most two high-part exponents per interval and the same two-term polynomial product.
solution_variants.complexity: O(nm^2 + m2^m) in a direct decomposition form

tags.evidence: The central operation is an XOR convolution of interval set polynomials, solved by XOR-FWT/FWHT and inverse FWT.
tags.description: Use the XOR variant of the fast Walsh-Hadamard transform to turn XOR convolution into pointwise multiplication.
tags.created_reason: The existing math/transform/fwt tag does not distinguish XOR convolution from AND/OR variants, while the repository already has a child tag for AND-FWT.

tags.evidence: The answer array is the n-fold XOR convolution of the interval indicator polynomials.
Each interval is represented as a set polynomial A_i, and the solution manipulates products of two-term set power series.
The simplification of each prefix FWT sum depends on the least significant set bit of the transform index k.
Full blocks cancel in pairs because the chosen lowbit contributes equal positive and negative signs.
The interval transform value is computed as a prefix contribution F([0,r]) minus F([0,l-1]).
All transform indices and signs are controlled by bitwise intersections and popcount parity.
The target values count sequences chosen from intervals under a global XOR condition.
